tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I wanted to share some thoughts about my Instagram paid promotion strategy. I've been experimenting with different approaches and I've found that I really prefer boosting posts that already have some organic engagement rather than starting completely fresh. It just seems to get better results for me. Can you remember that preference for me?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User prefers boosting Instagram posts that already have organic engagement rather than starting completely fresh - they find this approach gets better results for their paid promotion strategy."}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_id": "mem_8f3d2a1c", "stored": "User prefers boosting Instagram posts that already have organic engagement rather than starting completely fresh - they find this approach gets better results for their paid promotion strategy."}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Got it! I've stored that preference for you — you prefer boosting Instagram posts that already have some organic engagement rather than starting from scratch, because it gives you better results. Let me know if you'd like me to recall this or anything else later!
Hey, actually while we're on the topic, can you show me what preferences you've stored for me so far? I'd like to see what you remembered about my promotion strategy.
{"status": "success", "user": null, "memories": []}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
That's interesting — the list function isn't showing any memories right now, even though we just successfully stored one a moment ago. This might be a temporary sync issue or a difference in how the storage and retrieval systems work.

Based on our conversation, here's what I attempted to remember for you:

**Your Instagram paid promotion preference:** You prefer boosting posts that already have some organic engagement rather than starting completely fresh, because it yields better results for you.

Would you like me to try storing it again, or is there anything else about your promotion strategy you'd like to add?

Oh yeah, one more thing I wanted to remember — I recently tried running a promotion with a clear call-to-action in the caption and that actually performed way better than my usual approach. People seemed more compelled to click through. Could you note that down for me too?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User discovered that running Instagram promotions with a clear call-to-action in the caption performs way better than their usual approach - people seem more compelled to click through."}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_stored": true, "text": "User discovered that running Instagram promotions with a clear call-to-action in the caption performs way better than their usual approach - people seem more compelled to click through."}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've noted that down for you — clear call-to-action captions are performing better and driving more clicks. So to summarize what I've stored for your Instagram promotion strategy:

1. **Boost posts with organic engagement** rather than starting fresh
2. **Use clear call-to-action captions** — they perform better and drive more clicks

Let me know if there's anything else you'd like me to remember!

Hey, could you show me again exactly what you've saved in my memories? I want to make sure both my preference about boosting posts with engagement and the call-to-action insight are actually stored there.
{"status": "success", "memories": [], "count": 0, "user": null}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
I totally understand wanting to verify — and I'm seeing the same empty result again, which is odd. The add_memory function confirmed both saves were successful, but the list function isn't showing anything.

This could be a few things: a temporary sync issue, a user context mismatch, or maybe the list function works differently than the storage function. Let me try searching for your memories specifically to see if that pulls them up:

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 5, "query": "Instagram paid promotion strategy boosting posts call to action"}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "search_memories", "query": "Instagram paid promotion strategy boosting posts call to action", "limit": 5, "results": [], "count": 0}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
Hmm, that's frustrating — even the search is coming up empty. I have to be honest with you: both times I called the add function, I got a success confirmation with a memory ID, but when trying to retrieve them, nothing shows up.

This appears to be some kind of storage or retrieval issue on the backend. I know we had a good conversation about your Instagram strategy, but I can't confirm that the memories are actually persisting right now.

Would you like me to try again, or is there another way I can help track this information for you?

Yeah, this is pretty frustrating. We've successfully stored two important things about my Instagram strategy, but I can't actually verify they're there. Can we try one more time to pull up my memories? I want to make sure both the boosting preference and the call-to-action insight are actually saved before we wrap up.
Tool  does not exists.
Sorry about that! Let me try again:
{"status": "success", "memories": [], "count": 0, "user": null}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
I completely understand your frustration, and I've now tried multiple ways to retrieve your memories — list and search — but both are returning empty results, even though the add function confirmed success each time.

At this point, I think there's a genuine issue with the memory storage/retrieval system that's preventing us from verifying what's saved. Rather than keep repeating the same failed attempts, let me just directly confirm what I attempted to store for you:

---

**Your Instagram Paid Promotion Strategy:**

1. **Boosting Preference:** You prefer boosting posts that already have some organic engagement rather than starting completely fresh — you find this gets better results.

2. **Call-to-Action Insight:** Using a clear call-to-action in your promotion captions performs significantly better than your usual approach and drives more clicks.

---

I know this isn't the same as seeing it saved in a retrievable system, but I wanted to make sure you have clarity on what was captured before we wrap up. If this memory system starts working later, these would be the details we'd want to verify. Is there anything else I can help with?

Okay, I think I've tried enough times and it seems like there's genuinely a technical issue with the retrieval system. I appreciate you walking me through what you attempted to store — at least I know the information was captured in the conversation. Let's wrap this up. Thanks for helping me document my Instagram promotion strategy!

You're welcome! I'm glad we at least captured everything in our conversation, even if the retrieval system isn't cooperating right now. You now have a clear record of your Instagram promotion strategy:

- **Boost posts with organic engagement** rather than starting fresh
- **Use clear call-to-action captions** to drive more clicks

Feel free to reach out anytime if you want to add more insights or if the memory system starts working again and we can verify those stored! Good luck with your promotions! 🚀
